why_wrong: Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step: keeps the sin() term but multiplies it by zero so the out trace is flat and fails the reintegrated-phase check.
Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step: keeps a plausible sine on out but drives the wrapped-phase metric to zero so the metric trace fails the phase check.
Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step: adds a quarter-cycle phase shift to the output so the reintegrated-phase check fails at every sampled point.
Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step: flips the vco_gain sign so the output frequency moves the wrong way with vin and the reintegrated phase diverges.
Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step: halves the output amplitude leaving the sine shape plausible while calibrated amplitude checks fail.
